title: COVID and cancer link?
body:
This may be totally off-base, and I'm going by anecdotal evidence only (and very little, at that), but it's got me thinking about it enough that I figured I'd bring it up here. 

A few months ago, I had read somebody saying that they believed having COVID kind of "turns on" cancer that may have otherwise remained dormant. I didn't give it much thought at the time, but when I saw my hairdresser recently, she said that she knew several women who'd had cancer in the past but been in remission, gotten COVID, and then found themselves in stage 4 not long after. I realized that I'd had a pretty nasty case of COVID about two months before my DCIS was found in my mammogram, but mine was extensive enough that I highly doubt it developed in those two months. But I'd also had a mild case a couple of years prior. Anyway - what are your thoughts on this theory? Anybody here have a similar experience?
